slides.title: Object-Oriented Programming 🧊
slides.content:
C++ stands out over classic languages like C because C++ fully supports **Object-Oriented Programming** (OOP).

OOP is a programming strategy where you organize your program into "objects" that can interact with each other.

slides.title: Why OOP? 🤔
slides.content:
OOP is faster to execute, vastly easier to modify over time, and keeps your C++ code DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself).

By building reusable 'Class Blueprints', you can instantiate thousands of objects cleanly in memory.

quizData.question: OOP stands for Object-Oriented Programming.
quizData.explanation: Yes, OOP organizes everything as 'objects' with unique logic and properties.

slides.title: Classes vs Objects 🚗
slides.content:
A **Class** is the template, or blueprint.
An **Object** is an instance of a class.

When individual objects are created from a class, they inherit all the generic variables and functions, but can store unique data.

quizData.question: If 'Fruit' is a Class blueprint, what are 'Apple' and 'Banana'?

options.text: They are Objects instantiated from the Fruit Class

quizData.explanation: The Fruit class might define 'color' and 'taste'. The Apple object inherits these, setting color=Red, taste=Sweet.
